title: Genome-wide Interrogation of Protein-DNA Interactions in Mammalian Cells Using ChIPmentation
author: Xu, Ye, et al.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.instruction: There are many existing ChIP-seq protocols with different modifications. Many of them are lengthy and difficult to carry out. We have found ChIPmentation is the simplest and the most robust to implement in a molecular biology lab, especially for people who have already had the experience with the ChIP technique. The ChIPmentation method is modular, where it contains a ChIP module and a library preparation module. Experienced researchers can just stick to their own ChIP protocol and start following the procedures described here after washing the IP (i.e., step 13). People with no previous ChIP experience are recommended to follow the exact procedures described in this protocol. It is also recommended to read the full protocol before starting in order to get a feeling about the timing and work load in each step.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.instruction: Compared to other ChIP-seq methods, one advantage of ChIPmentation is its sensitivity. We routinely use 5ย ร 105 cells to profile histone modifications and 5ย ร 106 cells to study transcription factors. The minimum cell number required for a successful ChIPmentation experiment in our hands is 104 cells for histone modifications and 105 cells for transcription factors. However, it is worth noting that the number of cells required for a successful ChIPmentation experiment depends on many factors, such as the abundance of the protein/modification of interest and the efficiency of the antibody. The other major advantage of ChIPmentation is simplicity. Sequencing adapters are added by the transposase Tn5, and library PCR is performed immediately after reverse crosslinking and DNA purification. The third advantage is the cost. Only a small amount of Tn5 transposase is needed per library. Nowadays, we always use ChIPmentation even when the cell number is not a constraint, such as cell lines.

steps.title: DNA Purification and Library Preparation
steps.instruction: This section describes the procedures of DNA purification and library preparation. See Figureย 6 below for the schematic view of this section with PCR details.21.Purify DNA from both input and IP samples using the Qiagen minElute PCR Purification Kit according to manufacturerโs instructions. Elute the DNA in 11ย ฮผL Elution Buffer from the kit twice, which generally yields 20ย ฮผL DNA.Note: There is no need to quantify the DNA concentration at this stage. Use all for the next step.22.Setup the library PCR reaction per sample as follows:ReagentVolumePurified DNA from step 2120ย ฮผL10ย ฮผM S5xx Primer2.5ย ฮผL10ย ฮผM N7xx Primer2.5ย ฮผLNEBNext High-Fidelity 2ร PCR Master Mix25ย ฮผLTotal50ย ฮผL23.Run a pre-amplification PCR using the following condition:Pre-Amplification PCR Cycling ConditionsStepsTemperatureTimeCyclesGap fill-in72ยฐC5ย min1Initial Denaturation98ยฐC1ย min1Denaturation98ยฐC10 s4 cyclesAnnealing63ยฐC30 sExtension72ยฐC20 sHold10ยฐCForeverNote: The combination of S5xx and N7xx primers identifies a sample. Therefore, different samples should use different combinations of S5xx and N7xx primers. If you do not have many samples, it is recommended to use different N7xx primers, because the index in the N7xx primer is sequenced first on an Illumina machine.CRITICAL: Since the tagmentation process creates 9-bp gaps (see Figureย 5), the first step in the PCR should be 72ยฐC to allow the polymerase to fill in the gaps.24.After the pre-amplification, take out 9ย ฮผL of the reaction, and mix with 1ย ฮผL 10ร EvaGreen and perform a qPCR analysis to decide the optimal cycle number. Leave the rest 41ย ฮผL reaction on ice.25.Use the following cycling condition to perform a qPCR analysis, and monitor the amplification curve in linear scale.qPCR Cycling ConditionsStepsTemperatureTimeCyclesInitial Denaturation98ยฐC1ย min1Denaturation98ยฐC10 s35 cyclesAnnealing63ยฐC30 sExtension72ยฐC20ย s (acquire data)26.Determine the cycle number N, where the amplification curve reach half way of saturation. In the examples shown in the Figureย 7 below, Nย = 11, 14, and 14 for the three different samples.Figureย 7Amplification Plot of qPCRNote: The cycle number should be chosen at the exponential phase, before reaching saturation.27.Once the optimal cycle number is decided, amplify the rest 41ย ฮผL reaction for a further of N cycles, using the following condition:Post-Amplification PCR Cycling ConditionsStepsTemperatureTimeCyclesInitial Denaturation98ยฐC1ย min1Denaturation98ยฐC10 sN cyclesAnnealing63ยฐC30 sExtension72ยฐC20 sHold10ยฐCforeverCRITICAL: Typically, the number N is between 6 and 14, i.e., the total number of cycles needed is between 10 and 18 cycles depending on the number of cells and the abundance of the protein. See Troubleshooting 2.28.Purify the library PCR product using 1.2ร beads ratio using AmpureXP for PCR Purification beads or VAHTS DNA Clean Beads, according to manufacturerโs instructions. Elute the library using 30ย ฮผL Elution Buffer from the Qiagen minElute PCR Purification kit or 10ย mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0.Figureย 6Schematic View of Steps of DNA Purification and Library Purification

steps.title: Quantification and Statistical Analysis
steps.instruction: Unfortunately, a successful library preparation does not mean a successful ChIPmentation experiment. Some preliminary computational analysis on the data need to be performed to see if the ChIPmentation experiment is working or not.
